---
title: "Causal Inference for Latent Genetic Factors<br> Underlying Psychopathology"
authors: "Laura Sità"
format:
  revealjs:
    center: true
    embed-resources: true
    slide-number: true
    footer: "HandZone October 30th, 2025"
    theme: simple
    output-file: "latent-genetic-factors.html"
toc: true
execute:
  cache: false
  freeze: false
include-in-header:
  - text: |
      <style>
      /* Titolo slide di copertina */
      #title-slide .title {
        font-size: 1.5em;
        color: #900000;
      }

      /* Logo nella copertina */
      #title-slide::after {
        content: "";
        display: block;
        background-image: url("images/logo.png");
        background-repeat: no-repeat;
        background-position: center;
        background-size: contain;
        height: 150px;
        margin-top: 1.5em;
      }

      /* Titoli interni delle slide */
      .reveal h1, .reveal h2, .reveal h3 {
        color: #900000 !important;
      }

      /* Eventuale dimensione leggermente minore per h2 */
      .reveal h2 {
        font-size: 1.2em !important;
      }
      </style>
---

# Introduction

(Kotov et al., 2017)

# Problem

How do we find the latent factors?

- possible approach genomic SEM (Grotzinger et al., 2022)
- using the Non-negative matrix factorization (NMF), inspired by Landy et al., 2025

# Genomic SEM (Grotzinger et al., 2022)



# NMF 
```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE, message = FALSE, warning = FALSE)
library(knitr)
library(ggridges)
library(ggplot2)
```

```{r nmf}
#| echo: false
#| include: false
source("nmf.R")
```

## Data

NMF decomposes the full observed data matrix $\mathbf{Y} \in \mathbb{R}_{\ge 0}^{D \times N}$ into two lower-rank matrices:

factors $\mathbf{\lambda} \in \mathbb{R}_{\ge 0}^{D \times K}$

individual contributions $\mathbf{L} \in \mathbb{R}_{\ge 0}^{K \times N}$

We assume Poisson-generated data as follows:

$$
Y_{di} \sim \text{Poisson}\!\left(\sum_{k=1}^{K} \lambda_{dk} L_{ki}\right)
$$



```{r par}
#| include: true
#| echo: true 

set.seed(321)
N = 100 # number of i subjects 
D = 96 # number of observed variables 
K = 3 # number of latent factors
```

## Data simulation

```{r grafico}
#| echo: false
#| message: false

plotxslide <- ggplot(df, aes(x = C, y = as.factor(k), fill = Tr)) +
  geom_density_ridges(alpha = 0.6) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 18) +
  labs(
    x = "Latent outcome distribution", 
    y = "Latent dimension",
    fill = "Group"
  ) +
  theme(
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 22, margin = margin(t = 20)),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 22, margin = margin(r = 20)),
    axis.text = element_text(size = 20),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 29),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 22),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 24, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
    axis.text.y = element_text(margin = margin(r = 13))
  )

plotxslide

```

## Estimating the latent factors

NMF used in two algorithms 

```{r chat}
#| include: true
#| echo: true 

all_data_fit <- all_data(M, Tr, rank = 3, reference_P = true_P)
impute_and_stabilize_fit <- impute_and_stabilize(M, Tr, rank = 3, reference_P = true_P)

impute_and_stabilize_fit$Chat[, 1:6] # showing first 6 columns

```
##  
```{r graficoChatF1}
#| echo: false

plot_latent_factor(1, true_C, all_data_fit, impute_and_stabilize_fit)

```

## 
```{r graficoChatF2}
#| echo: false

plot_latent_factor(2, true_C, all_data_fit, impute_and_stabilize_fit)

```

## 
```{r graficoChatF3}
#| echo: false

plot_latent_factor(3, true_C, all_data_fit, impute_and_stabilize_fit)

```

# Materials 
All the materials are available in the `project` folder inside the following repository <a href="https://github.com/laurasitaunipd/handzone.git" target="_blank">laurasitaunipd/handzone</a>.

# Bibliography

Grotzinger, A. D., Mallard, T. T., Akingbuwa, W. A., Ip, H. F., Adams, M. J., Lewis, C. M., ... & Nivard, M. G. (2022). Genetic architecture of 11 major psychiatric disorders at biobehavioral, functional genomic and molecular genetic levels of analysis. Nature genetics, 54(5), 548-559.

Kotov, R., Krueger, R. F., Watson, D., Achenbach, T. M., Althoff, R. R., Bagby, R. M., ... & Zimmerman, M. (2017). The Hierarchical Taxonomy of Psychopathology (HiTOP): A dimensional alternative to traditional nosologies. Journal of abnormal psychology, 126(4), 454.

Landy, J. M., Zorzetto, D., De Vito, R., & Parmigiani, G. (2025). Causal Inference for Latent Outcomes Learned with Factor Models. arXiv preprint arXiv:2506.20549.
